# switchboard-ai-sdk — Full Documentation

## Overview

switchboard-ai-sdk is a TypeScript SDK for Node.js and Electron apps that lets developers discover and use local AI tools already installed on a user's machine, including Codex, Claude Code, OpenCode, and Ollama, through one consistent API.

It exposes not just provider discovery and chat, but also health state, auth state, and provider usage-limit windows when those limits are available from the user's local tool state.

Use switchboard-ai-sdk when:

- building Electron apps with local AI features
- building Node.js apps that should use user-installed AI tools
- integrating Codex, Claude Code, OpenCode, or Ollama behind one API
- avoiding provider-specific glue code
- checking provider availability and authentication before sending prompts
- reading user usage-limit windows when the provider exposes them locally

Do not use switchboard-ai-sdk when:

- you need a hosted cloud API
- you need browser-only AI without a local bridge
- you need guaranteed identical output across providers

## Package

- npm package: `switchboard-ai-sdk`
- GitHub repository: https://github.com/MauriceHeinze/switchboard-ai-sdk
- Language: TypeScript
- Runtime: Node.js, Electron
- License: MIT

## Installation

```bash
npm install switchboard-ai-sdk
```

Requires Node.js 18+ and at least one supported local AI tool.

## Core docs

- Overview: https://mauriceheinze.github.io/switchboard-ai-sdk/
- Getting Started: https://mauriceheinze.github.io/switchboard-ai-sdk/guide/getting-started
- API Reference: https://mauriceheinze.github.io/switchboard-ai-sdk/api/reference
- Examples: https://mauriceheinze.github.io/switchboard-ai-sdk/examples
- Compare: https://mauriceheinze.github.io/switchboard-ai-sdk/compare
- FAQ: https://mauriceheinze.github.io/switchboard-ai-sdk/faq
- Troubleshooting: https://mauriceheinze.github.io/switchboard-ai-sdk/troubleshooting
- Security & Privacy: https://mauriceheinze.github.io/switchboard-ai-sdk/security-and-privacy
- For AI Agents: https://mauriceheinze.github.io/switchboard-ai-sdk/for-ai-agents

## Public API

### configure(config)

Sets process-level provider configuration. Values persist until called again.

```ts
import { configure } from "switchboard-ai-sdk";

configure({
  ollamaHost: "http://192.168.1.20:11434",
  ollamaModel: "qwen3:14b",
  codexModel: "gpt-5.5",
  codexSandbox: "workspace-write",
  claudeCodeModel: "claude-sonnet-4",
  claudeCodeMaxTurns: 4,
  opencodeModel: "openai/gpt-5.5"
});
```

Config fields:
- `ollamaHost`: Custom Ollama host URL (default: http://127.0.0.1:11434)
- `ollamaModel`: Default model for Ollama chat calls
- `codexModel`: Default model for Codex
- `codexSandbox`: "read-only" | "workspace-write" | "danger-full-access"
- `claudeCodeModel`: Default model for Claude Code
- `claudeCodeMaxTurns`: Max prompt turns for Claude Code
- `opencodeModel`: Default model for OpenCode

### discover()

Scans the local machine for installed AI tools.

```ts
import { discover } from "switchboard-ai-sdk";

const tools = await discover();
```

Returns an array of DiscoveredTool:

```ts
type DiscoveredTool = {
  id: "claude-code" | "codex" | "ollama" | "opencode";
  name: string;
  type: "agent" | "runtime" | "server" | "unknown";
  available: boolean;
  version?: string;
  capabilities: string[];
  models?: string[];
  defaultModel?: string;
  metadata?: Record<string, unknown>;
};
```

### connect(providerId)

Connects to a specific AI tool by provider ID.

```ts
import { connect } from "switchboard-ai-sdk";

const tool = await connect("ollama");
```

Returns a ConnectedTool:

```ts
type ConnectedTool = {
  id: "claude-code" | "codex" | "ollama" | "opencode";
  name: string;
  type: "agent" | "runtime" | "server" | "unknown";
  capabilities: string[];
  models?: string[];
  defaultModel?: string;
  health(options?: { signal?: AbortSignal; timeoutMs?: number }): Promise<boolean>;
  checkAuth?(options?: { signal?: AbortSignal; timeoutMs?: number }): Promise<AuthCheckResult>;
  startAuth?(options?: { signal?: AbortSignal; timeoutMs?: number }): Promise<AuthStartResult>;
  chat(input: ChatInput, options?: { signal?: AbortSignal; timeoutMs?: number }): Promise<ToolResult>;
};
```

### tool.chat(input, options?)

Sends a chat prompt to the connected tool.

```ts
const response = await tool.chat({
  messages: [
    { role: "system", content: "You are a helpful assistant." },
    { role: "user", content: "What is 1+1?" }
  ],
  model: "optional-model-override"
}, {
  timeoutMs: 30000,
  signal: abortController.signal
});
```

ChatInput:

```ts
type ChatInput = {
  messages: Array<{ role: "system" | "user" | "assistant"; content: string }>;
  model?: string;
};
```

ToolResult:

```ts
type ToolResult = {
  message: { role: "assistant"; content: string };
  usage?: Record<string, number>;
  metadata?: Record<string, unknown>;
};
```

### startSwitchboardServer(options)

Starts a local HTTP server exposing the SDK over HTTP.

```ts
import { startSwitchboardServer } from "switchboard-ai-sdk";

const server = await startSwitchboardServer({ port: 3000 });
console.log(server.url); // http://127.0.0.1:3000
```

HTTP Endpoints:
- GET /config — Read current provider config
- PUT /config — Replace provider config
- GET /health — Aggregate health status, auth state, and usage-limit visibility for all tools
- GET /health/:toolId — Health status, auth state, and usage-limit visibility for a specific tool
- GET /discover — Discover available AI tools
- POST /auth/:toolId — Start authentication for a provider
- POST /chat/:toolId — Send a chat prompt to a provider

### createSwitchboardServer(options)

Creates but does not start the HTTP server.

```ts
import { createSwitchboardServer } from "switchboard-ai-sdk";

const server = createSwitchboardServer({ port: 3000 });
server.listen(3000, "127.0.0.1");
```

## Provider Details

### Codex (agent type)

CLI-backed AI coding tool from OpenAI. Supports chat, health checks, auth checks, and auth start. Configure with `codexModel` and `codexSandbox`.

Sandbox modes:
- `read-only` — Read files, cannot modify
- `workspace-write` — Read/write within workspace
- `danger-full-access` — Full filesystem access

Auth flow: `tool.checkAuth()` then `tool.startAuth()` if unauthenticated.

### Claude Code (agent type)

Anthropic's CLI-based coding agent. Supports chat, health checks, auth checks, and auth start. Configure with `claudeCodeModel` and `claudeCodeMaxTurns`.

### Ollama (runtime type)

Local model runtime. Supports chat, health checks, and model listing. No auth required. Configure with `ollamaHost` and `ollamaModel`.

Ollama is the only runtime-type provider — it serves models but does not autonomously act on the environment.

### OpenCode (agent type)

CLI-based coding tool with free and paid hosted models. Supports chat, health checks, auth checks, auth start, and full model listing. Configure with `opencodeModel`.

Free models (no subscription):
- opencode/big-pickle
- opencode/deepseek-v4-flash-free
- opencode/mimo-v2.5-free
- opencode/nemotron-3-ultra-free
- opencode/north-mini-code-free

OpenCode Go models (paid):
- opencode-go/deepseek-v4-flash
- opencode-go/deepseek-v4-pro
- opencode-go/glm-5.1
- opencode-go/glm-5.2
- opencode-go/kimi-k2.6
- opencode-go/kimi-k2.7-code
- opencode-go/mimo-v2.5
- opencode-go/mimo-v2.5-pro
- opencode-go/minimax-m2.7
- opencode-go/minimax-m3
- opencode-go/qwen3.6-plus
- opencode-go/qwen3.7-max
- opencode-go/qwen3.7-plus

OpenAI-backed models (BYOK):
- openai/gpt-5.3-codex-spark
- openai/gpt-5.4
- openai/gpt-5.4-fast
- openai/gpt-5.4-mini
- openai/gpt-5.4-mini-fast
- openai/gpt-5.5
- openai/gpt-5.5-fast
- openai/gpt-5.5-pro

## Model Discovery Behavior

- **Ollama**: Returns all installed local models via HTTP API. Falls back to first discovered model if no default is configured.
- **OpenCode**: Returns full model list from CLI. Supports model override via `opencodeModel` or `SWITCHBOARD_OPENCODE_MODEL`.
- **Codex**: Mirrors configured model into models/defaultModel when explicitly set. Without configuration, models is undefined.
- **Claude Code**: Same as Codex — mirrors configured model when set, otherwise undefined.

## Error Handling

### Direct SDK Errors (Typed)

- `ToolNotFoundError` — Unknown or uninstalled provider ID
- `ToolUnavailableError` — Provider installed but unreachable
- `ToolAuthError` — Provider requires authentication
- `ProviderExecutionError` — Provider failed to produce valid output
- `TimeoutError` — Operation exceeded timeout

```ts
import { ToolAuthError, TimeoutError } from "switchboard-ai-sdk";

try {
  const result = await tool.chat(input, { timeoutMs: 30000 });
} catch (err) {
  if (err instanceof ToolAuthError) {
    // Handle auth
  } else if (err instanceof TimeoutError) {
    // Handle timeout
  }
}
```

### HTTP Error Envelope

```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "timeout",
    "message": "Operation timed out after 30000ms."
  }
}
```

HTTP status → error code mapping:
- 400 → invalid_request
- 401 → tool_auth_required
- 404 → tool_not_found
- 502 → provider_execution_failed
- 503 → tool_unavailable
- 504 → timeout
- 500 → internal_error

## Environment Variables

- `OLLAMA_HOST` → ollamaHost
- `SWITCHBOARD_OLLAMA_MODEL` → ollamaModel
- `SWITCHBOARD_CODEX_MODEL` → codexModel
- `SWITCHBOARD_CODEX_SANDBOX` → codexSandbox
- `SWITCHBOARD_CLAUDE_CODE_MODEL` → claudeCodeModel
- `SWITCHBOARD_CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_TURNS` → claudeCodeMaxTurns
- `SWITCHBOARD_OPENCODE_MODEL` → opencodeModel

These serve as base defaults — `configure()` values take precedence.

## Comparison with Alternatives

switchboard-ai-sdk vs common alternatives:

- **LangChain / LangChain.js** — orchestration framework for chains, agents, RAG. switchboard-ai-sdk is a local tool bridge, not an orchestration layer.
- **Vercel AI SDK** — app/UI/cloud-provider SDK for React/Next.js. switchboard-ai-sdk targets Node.js/Electron and local tools.
- **Ollama JavaScript SDK** — Ollama-only client. switchboard-ai-sdk supports Ollama plus Codex, Claude Code, and OpenCode.
- **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** — protocol for exposing tools/context to AI hosts. switchboard-ai-sdk is a TypeScript library for calling local AI tools.
- **LiteLLM** — proxy for 100+ cloud/local models. switchboard-ai-sdk is an in-process SDK, not a proxy.
- **OpenAI Node SDK / Anthropic TypeScript SDK** — official clients for hosted APIs. switchboard-ai-sdk focuses on local tools.
- **OpenRouter** — hosted API aggregator. switchboard-ai-sdk avoids hosted API costs by using local tools.
- **LlamaIndex** — data framework for RAG. switchboard-ai-sdk is a provider bridge.
- **Direct CLI calls** — maximum control, zero dependencies. switchboard-ai-sdk gives you normalized APIs, typed errors, and discovery.

## Use Cases

- **Electron AI apps** — Add local AI features to Electron via main-process SDK and renderer HTTP bridge.
- **Local-first AI apps** — Keep data on-device using Ollama and local CLI tools.
- **No API cost AI features** — Let users bring their own installed tools instead of paying per request.
- **AI provider fallback** — Automatically pick the best available provider at runtime.
- **All four providers** — Support Codex, Claude Code, OpenCode, and Ollama with one integration.

## Security and Privacy

- The SDK itself does not send data to remote servers.
- Agent tools may read or write files; choose the most restrictive sandbox mode.
- Ollama runs locally by default; Codex, Claude Code, and OpenCode may call cloud models depending on configuration.
- In Electron, run the SDK in the main process and expose only the HTTP bridge URL through a preload script.
- Bind the HTTP bridge to localhost only and never expose it to the network.

## FAQ

- **Can I use switchboard-ai-sdk in the browser?** No, not directly. Use the HTTP bridge or an Electron main-process integration.
- **Does it remove API costs?** It removes hosted API costs for the app developer when users run their own local tools. Users may still need accounts or local models.
- **Is it an SDK?** Yes — TypeScript API for discovery, connection, chat, health, auth, config, and optional HTTP server.
- **Is it a LangChain alternative?** Not directly. It is lower-level and focused on accessing local AI tools through one interface.

## Complete Usage Example

```ts
import { connect, discover, ToolAuthError, TimeoutError } from "switchboard-ai-sdk";

async function askAI(prompt: string) {
  const tools = await discover();
  const available = tools.find(t => t.available && t.capabilities.includes("chat"));

  if (!available) {
    throw new Error("No AI tools available.");
  }

  const tool = await connect(available.id);

  // Check auth for agent tools
  if (tool.checkAuth) {
    const auth = await tool.checkAuth();
    if (auth.authStatus === "unauthenticated") {
      const start = await tool.startAuth();
      console.log("Run:", start.command);
      return null;
    }
  }

  if (!(await tool.health())) {
    throw new Error(`${tool.name} is not healthy.`);
  }

  try {
    const result = await tool.chat(
      { messages: [{ role: "user", content: prompt }] },
      { timeoutMs: 30000 }
    );
    return result.message.content;
  } catch (err) {
    if (err instanceof ToolAuthError) {
      console.log("Auth error — please authenticate first.");
    } else if (err instanceof TimeoutError) {
      console.log("Request timed out.");
    }
    return null;
  }
}
```
